---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
source("source.R")
```

## Load Data

```{r}
bundle <- readRDS("data/bundle.rds")

data_all            <- bundle$data_all
items               <- bundle$dict
accept_vars         <- bundle$acceptability_vars
accept_service_vars <- bundle$accept_service_vars
accept_info_vars    <- bundle$accept_info_vars
accept_data_vars    <- bundle$accept_data_vars
privacy_vars        <- bundle$privacy_vars
country_var         <- bundle$country_var

stopifnot(country_var %in% names(data_all))
stopifnot(all(accept_vars %in% names(data_all)))

# Numeric version of acceptability items (used throughout)
acc_numeric <- data_all %>%
  select(all_of(accept_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ as.numeric(.x)))
```

```{r}
head (data_all)
```

```{r}
colnames(data_all)
```

## Block 1: Sample Overview

```{r}
# Dimensions and summary
dim(data_all)
summary(data_all)
```

```{r}
# N per country
data_all %>%
  count(country_code, name = "N") %>%
  arrange(desc(N))
```

```{r}
# Demographic snapshot by country
data_all %>%
  group_by(country_code) %>%
  summarise(
    N            = n(),
    mean_age     = round(mean(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    pct_female   = round(mean(gender == "female", na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
    pct_high_edu = round(mean(education_split == "high", na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1)
  )
```

## Block 2: Missing Data

```{r}
miss_tbl <- tibble(
  variable = names(data_all),
  miss_pct = sapply(data_all, function(x) mean(is.na(x)) * 100)
) %>%
  arrange(desc(miss_pct))

# Show only variables with missing values
miss_tbl %>% filter(miss_pct > 0)
```

## Block 3: Demographic Visuals

```{r}
# N by country
n_by_country <- data_all %>%
  count(country_code, name = "N") %>%
  arrange(desc(N))

n_by_country
```

```{r}
# Age distribution by country
ggplot(data_all, aes(age, fill = country_code)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_country() +
  labs(
    title   = "Age Distribution by Country",
    x       = "Age",
    y       = "Count"
  )
```

```{r}
# Education by country
ggplot(data_all, aes(education_split, fill = country_code)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_country() +
  labs(
    title   = "Education by Country",
    x       = NULL,
    y       = "Count"
  )
```

## Block 4: Distributional Properties

```{r}
# Skewness and kurtosis per item
skew_kurt <- psych::describe(acc_numeric) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  select(mean, sd, skew, kurtosis) %>%
  mutate(
    item      = rownames(.),
    dimension = case_when(
      item %in% accept_service_vars ~ "Service",
      item %in% accept_info_vars    ~ "Information",
      item %in% accept_data_vars    ~ "Data"
    )
  )
```

```{r}
# Skewness/kurtosis table
skew_kurt %>%
  select(item, dimension, mean, sd, skew, kurtosis) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2))) %>%
  arrange(dimension, mean)
```

```{r}
# Skewness plot
ggplot(skew_kurt, aes(x = skew, y = reorder(item, skew), color = dimension)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_vline(xintercept =  0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  geom_vline(xintercept =  1, linetype = "dotted",
             color = unname(palette_thesis["rose"])) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = -1, linetype = "dotted",
             color = unname(palette_thesis["rose"])) +
  scale_colour_dimension() +
  labs(
    title   = "Skewness of Acceptability Items by Dimension",
    x       = "Skewness",
    y       = NULL,
    color   = "Dimension"
  )
```

```{r}
# Count items with substantial skewness
skew_kurt %>% filter(skew > 0.5) %>% nrow()
```

## Block 5: Acceptability Overview

```{r}
# Reshape to long format
accept_long <- data_all %>%
  select(all_of(accept_vars)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "item", values_to = "response") %>%
  filter(!is.na(response)) %>%
  mutate(
    item     = factor(item, levels = rev(accept_vars)),
    response = factor(as.character(response),
                      levels = c("Not acceptable at all", "Not very acceptable",
                                 "Somewhat acceptable", "Very acceptable"),
                      ordered = TRUE)
  )

accept_summary <- accept_long %>%
  count(item, response, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(item) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
accept_summary
```

```{r}
# Stacked bar: full acceptability overview
ggplot(accept_summary, aes(x = pct, y = item, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_response() +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title   = "Acceptability of Data Use Across Contexts",
    x       = "Share of respondents",
    y       = NULL,
    fill    = "Response"
  )
```

```{r}
# Commercial vs political personalization by country
item_palette <- c(
  "commercials"         = unname(palette_thesis["teal"]),
  "political_campaigns" = unname(palette_thesis["rose"])
)

data_all %>%
  select(country_code, commercials, political_campaigns) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(commercials, political_campaigns),
               names_to = "item", values_to = "response") %>%
  filter(!is.na(response)) %>%
  group_by(country_code, item, response) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(country_code, item) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = response, y = pct, fill = item)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ country_code) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = item_palette, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title   = "Commercial vs Political Personalization by Country",
    x       = NULL,
    y       = "Share of respondents",
    fill    = "Item"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
# Mean acceptability by country and item
accept_num <- data_all %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(accept_vars), ~ as.numeric(.x)))

country_item_means <- accept_num %>%
  select(country_code, all_of(accept_vars)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(accept_vars),
               names_to = "item", values_to = "score") %>%
  group_by(country_code, item) %>%
  summarise(mean_score = mean(score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(country_item_means,
       aes(x = mean_score, y = item, colour = country_code)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_colour_country() +
  labs(
    title   = "Average Acceptability by Country (1 = Not acceptable, 4 = Very acceptable)",
    x       = "Mean acceptability",
    y       = NULL,
    colour  = "Country"
  ) + 
  theme(
      axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1, size = 18),
      axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.title   = element_text(size = 20),
      plot.title   = element_text(size = 20),
      legend.text   = element_text(size = 16),
      legend.title  = element_text(size = 18)
    )
```

## Block 6: Relationships Motivating Models

```{r}
# Privacy concern distribution
data_all %>%
  count(concern_privacy) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = concern_privacy, y = pct)) +
  geom_col(fill = unname(palette_thesis["mauve"]), alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 1)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(
    title   = "Distribution of Privacy Concern",
    x       = "Privacy Concern",
    y       = "Share of respondents"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1, size = 13),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.title   = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 16)
  )
```

```{r}
# Privacy concern vs mean acceptability
data_all %>%
  mutate(
    mean_accept = rowMeans(
      select(., all_of(accept_vars)) %>%
        mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric)),
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(concern_privacy)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = concern_privacy, y = mean_accept)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = unname(palette_thesis["teal"]),
               alpha = 0.6, outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = TRUE,
              color = unname(palette_thesis["rose"]),
              linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title   = "Privacy Concern vs Mean Acceptability",
    x       = "Privacy Concern",
    y       = "Mean Acceptability (1-4)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1, size = 13),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.title   = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 16)
  )
```

```{r}
# Political personalization acceptability by privacy concern level
data_all %>%
  select(concern_privacy, political_campaigns) %>%
  filter(!is.na(political_campaigns)) %>%
  count(concern_privacy, political_campaigns) %>%
  group_by(concern_privacy) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = concern_privacy, y = pct, fill = political_campaigns)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_response() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title   = "Political Personalization Acceptability by Privacy Concern",
    x       = "Privacy Concern",
    y       = "Share",
    fill    = "Acceptability"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
names(data_all)[grep("rec|sys", names(data_all), ignore.case = TRUE)]
```


```{r}
# Algorithmic awareness by country
# Note: RecSys becomes "recsys" after janitor::make_clean_names
awareness_vars <- c("ai", "algorithms", "ml", "rec_sys", "targeted")

data_all %>%
  select(country_code, all_of(awareness_vars)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(awareness_vars),
               names_to = "term", values_to = "aware") %>%
  mutate(
    aware = as.numeric(aware == TRUE),
    term  = recode(term,
      "ai"         = "Artificial Intelligence",
      "algorithms" = "Algorithms",
      "ml"         = "Machine Learning",
      "rec_sys"     = "Recommender Systems",
      "targeted"   = "Targeted Advertising"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(country_code, term) %>%
  summarise(pct_aware = mean(aware, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(term, pct_aware), y = pct_aware, fill = country_code)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_country() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title   = "Algorithmic Awareness by Country",
    x       = NULL,
    y       = "Share aware",
    fill    = "Country"
  )
```

```{r}
# AI familiarity vs mean acceptability per context
data_all %>%
  group_by(ai) %>%
  summarise(
    `Political campaigns`       = mean(as.numeric(political_campaigns), na.rm = TRUE),
    `Customised search results` = mean(as.numeric(customised_search_results), na.rm = TRUE),
    `Entertainment recs`        = mean(as.numeric(personalised_entertainment_recommendations), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
# Political leaning vs acceptability
data_all %>%
  group_by(political_split) %>%
  summarise(
    `Political campaigns` = mean(as.numeric(political_campaigns), na.rm = TRUE),
    `Commercials`         = mean(as.numeric(commercials), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

## Block 7: Correlation Structure

```{r}
poly_corr <- psych::polychoric(acc_numeric)$rho

corr_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(poly_corr)) %>%
  rename(item1 = Var1, item2 = Var2, r = Freq)
```

```{r}
corr_long
```

```{r}
# Within- vs between-dimension correlation summary
get_mean_corr <- function(corr_matrix, items1, items2 = NULL) {
  if (is.null(items2)) items2 <- items1
  subset_corr <- corr_matrix[items1, items2]
  if (identical(items1, items2)) {
    subset_corr[lower.tri(subset_corr, diag = TRUE)] <- NA
  }
  mean(subset_corr, na.rm = TRUE)
}

tibble(
  Comparison = c(
    "Within: Service",
    "Within: Information",
    "Within: Data",
    "Cross: Service x Information",
    "Cross: Service x Data",
    "Cross: Information x Data"
  ),
  Mean_r = round(c(
    get_mean_corr(poly_corr, accept_service_vars),
    get_mean_corr(poly_corr, accept_info_vars),
    get_mean_corr(poly_corr, accept_data_vars),
    get_mean_corr(poly_corr, accept_service_vars, accept_info_vars),
    get_mean_corr(poly_corr, accept_service_vars, accept_data_vars),
    get_mean_corr(poly_corr, accept_info_vars,    accept_data_vars)
  ), 2)
)

```

```{r}
# Polychoric correlation heatmap
ggplot(corr_long, aes(x = item1, y = item2, fill = r)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(limits   = c(-1, 1),
                       low      = unname(palette_thesis["rose"]),
                       mid      = "white",
                       high     = unname(palette_thesis["teal"])) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(
    title   = "Polychoric Correlation Heatmap (Acceptability Items)",
    x       = NULL,
    y       = NULL,
    fill    = "\u03c1"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 18),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 20),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20)
  )
```

## Block 8: Internal Consistency and Factor Structure

```{r}
# Cronbach alpha per dimension
alpha_overall <- psych::alpha(acc_numeric)$total$raw_alpha
alpha_service <- psych::alpha(acc_numeric %>% select(all_of(accept_service_vars)))$total$raw_alpha
alpha_info    <- psych::alpha(acc_numeric %>% select(all_of(accept_info_vars)))$total$raw_alpha
alpha_data    <- psych::alpha(acc_numeric %>% select(all_of(accept_data_vars)))$total$raw_alpha

tibble(
  Scale          = c("Overall", "Service", "Information", "Data"),
  N_items        = c(length(accept_vars), length(accept_service_vars),
                     length(accept_info_vars), length(accept_data_vars)),
  Cronbach_alpha = round(c(alpha_overall, alpha_service,
                            alpha_info, alpha_data), 3)
)
```

```{r}
# Parallel analysis: empirical justification for number of factors
set.seed(42)
fa_parallel <- psych::fa.parallel(
  acc_numeric,
  fa     = "fa",
  n.iter = 20,
  main   = "Parallel Analysis: Number of Factors"
)
```

```{r}
# Exploratory factor analysis: check if 3-factor structure emerges
set.seed(42)
fa_result <- psych::fa(
  acc_numeric,
  nfactors = 3,
  rotate   = "oblimin",
  fm       = "ml"
)

# Item loadings
print(fa_result$loadings, cutoff = 0.3)

# Variance explained
cat("\nVariance explained:\n")
print(fa_result$Vx)

# Factor correlations
cat("\nFactor correlations:\n")
print(fa_result$Phi)
```
